# Play to Understand

> Quiet interactive educational games about complex ideas. Each game is short
> (10–20 minutes) and turns concepts from philosophy, history, ethics,
> psychology, and biology into something you can play, test, and feel.
> New games drop about once a month — sometimes less, never more.

The site is a one-person project by Alex Shlykov. It is intentionally small:
no analytics, no CMS, no funnel.

## Games

- [The Choice](https://thechoice.quest/): An interactive journey through the foundations of moral philosophy. Tags: ethics, philosophy.
- [The Evolution of Civilizations](https://evolutionofcivilizations.earth/): Trace the forces that shaped the rise and fall of civilizations. Tags: history.
- [How the Brain Shapes Behavior](https://buildtounderstand.dev/thebrain/): Discover which brain systems drive your everyday decisions. Tags: biology, psychology.

## Topics

- ethics
- philosophy
- history
- biology
- psychology

## Subscribe & support

- Newsletter (Substack/Blog): https://buildtounderstand.substack.com/
- Patreon: https://patreon.com/buildtounderstand

## Optional

- Site: https://playtounderstand.com/
- About the maker: Alex Shlykov — independent designer/developer, builds small interactive educational games.
